hook.text: Facebook needed to search inboxes across billions of messages. Cassandra was built for massive write throughput.

buildup.text: Cassandra is leaderless. Every node can accept writes. Data is replicated to configurable numbers of nodes.

discovery.text: It uses consistent hashing for partitioning and tunable consistency per query. Read ONE, write QUORUM — your choice.

twist.text: Cassandra has no joins and limited secondary indexes. Model your data around your queries, not the other way.

climax.text: Apple, Netflix, and Discord use Cassandra for massive-scale workloads. Trillions of rows, petabytes of data.

punchline.text: Cassandra: leaderless, tunable consistency, built for massive write throughput.

quiz.question: What is a key limitation when using Cassandra?
options: It has no joins and limited secondary indexes, requiring query-driven data modeling
It can only store up to 1 million rows per table
Cassandra only supports eventual consistency, never strong
